title: Interfaces, Dynamic Types, Errors, and API Boundaries · Go Semantics Case: Type Assertions Switches
summary: A notification service must accept narrow capabilities, preserve error causes, and distinguish nil interfaces from typed nil values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go type assertions switches increment in a capability-oriented notification boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.
Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
